new_classes.definition_en: An alcoholic fermentation and distillation process whose feedstock is fruit (including apples and pears for calvados), yielding a fruit spirit (Obstbrand/Obstbranntwein) as defined under CN subheading 2208 90 71.

new_classes.label_en: fruit feedstock (fermentation/distillation feedstock, any fruit including apples and pears)
new_classes.label_de: Obstausgangsstoff (Vergärungs-/Destillationsausgangsstoff, beliebiges Obst einschließlich Äpfel und Birnen)
new_classes.definition_en: A material entity consisting of fruit (of any kind, including apples and pears used for calvados production) that serves as the primary feedstock for fermentation and distillation to produce a fruit spirit classified under CN 2208 90 71.

new_classes.label_en: fruit spirit (distilled from fruit) in containers ≤ 2 L (CN 2208 90 71)
new_classes.label_de: Obstbranntwein in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von 2 l oder weniger (KN 2208 90 71)
new_classes.definition_en: A spirituous beverage of heading 2208 that is distilled from fruit (including calvados), presented in containers of 2 litres or less. This subheading covers all fruit spirits distilled from fruit other than those classified under the plum/pear/cherry brandy subheadings, and explicitly includes calvados.

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the key distinguishing criteria for CN 2208 90 71: (1) the product is a spirituous beverage of heading 2208; (2) it is distilled from fruit; (3) it is presented in containers ≤ 2 L; (4) it includes calvados. The new FruitDistillationProcess class generalises over the existing OtherFruitFermentationAndDistillationProcess (which excludes plums/pears/cherries) to cover all fruit distillation including calvados (apple-based). The container size constraint is expressed via the existing SmallContainer TBox class. A minor gap remains: the legal note cross-references explanatory note 22089048 for the definition of 'fruit spirit', and the precise boundary between this subheading and the plum/pear/cherry brandy subheadings (22089033/38) is not formally axiomatised as a complement restriction here. Full coverage of the calvados identity (apple/pear origin) would require a named Calvados subclass, which is not strictly necessary for discrimination at this level. Coverage is therefore assessed at 0.85.
